data.title: Tesla Stock Rises Premarket: Lawmakers Urge Trump To Keep Chinese EV Rivals Out Of American Market
data.description:
Key points:
  • More than 70 House Democrats, led by Debbie Dingell and Ro Khanna, reportedly called for maintaining restrictions on Chinese automakers.
  • The push comes ahead of Trump’s meeting with Xi Jinping and follows similar appeals from senators and major auto-industry groups.
  • China suspended new robotaxi license approvals after about 200 Baidu Apollo Go vehicles stopped mid-traffic in Wuhan.

Shares of Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) rose 0.3% in premarket trading on Wednesday after U.S. lawmakers urged U.S. President Donald Trump to keep Chinese EV rivals out of America, spotlighting intensifying U.S.-China competition in the EV race.

TSLA stock is poised to snap three straight months of losses, rising just over 1% so far in April.

Senators Join Push To Block Chinese Vehicles

On Tuesday, over 70 Democratic lawmakers in the U.S. House called on Trump to maintain restrictions preventing Chinese automakers from entering the American market, citing national security concerns over connected-vehicle data and rising competitive pressure on the domestic auto industry, Reuters reported.

The letter, led by Debbie Dingell and Ro Khanna, comes ahead of Trump’s meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping next month and follows similar appeals from senators and major auto-industry groups in recent weeks.

While U.S. officials said that national-security safeguards remain a priority, China’s embassy urged Washington to avoid “discriminatory” restrictions. Republican Senator Bernie Moreno separately said he plans legislation to permanently block Chinese vehicles from entering the U.S. market.

China Steps Up Scrutiny Of Driverless Fleets

At the same time, regulatory developments in China are adding another layer of uncertainty to the autonomous-driving space, which is key to Tesla’s long-term strategy.

Chinese authorities recently suspended new robotaxi license approvals after 200 driverless vehicles operating under Baidu’s Apollo Go program stopped mid-traffic in Wuhan in a major traffic incident. Four central government agencies subsequently ordered a comprehensive safety self-inspection across major autonomous-driving companies following the outage. Local police said that the disruption was due to a system failure, according to a Nikkei report.

The review could temporarily weigh on expectations for large-scale robotaxi deployment, even as regulators continue supporting autonomous driving over the longer term.

China: Key Battleground For Tesla 

Tesla continues to navigate regulatory and competitive pressures in China, its second-largest market and home to its largest global export hub in Shanghai.

The company has been working with Baidu to improve navigation-map integration for its Full Self-Driving Version (FSD) 13 software as it adapts the system to local road conditions under strict data-localization requirements. Earlier this year, Tesla also launched a local AI training center in China to develop its assisted-driving capabilities tailored to domestic traffic environments.

CEO Elon Musk recently said that the “limiting factor” for Tesla in China remains production output at its Shanghai facility ahead of broader supervised FSD approval in the country.

Tesla’s share of China’s EV market declined last year to 10.4% from 11.7% in 2023 as domestic rivals including Nio, BYD, XPeng and Li Auto expand their advanced driver-assistance capabilities across their lineups, often bundling them at lower price points.

How Do Retail Traders Feel About TSLA?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment for Tesla was ‘extremely bullish’ amid ‘extremely high’ message volume.

One user called TSLA a “major EV leader where growth expectations, innovation, and sentiment can move the stock quickly”

https://stocktwits.com/ChartStudioControl/message/651708809

Another user weighed in on Tesla’s stock price, saying, “for all the pump it’s just treading water.”

https://stocktwits.com/Blackline/message/651703789

So far this year, TSLA stock has lagged its “Magnificent Seven” peers, making it the group’s worst performer, with a 16% decline.

data.description:

Net profit reached 86.941 billion yuan ($12.73 billion) in the first quarter this year, up from 84.156 billion yuan a year earlier.

data.title: ICBC Posts Higher Quarterly Net Profit
data.description:
(08:31 GMT) *Industrial & Commercial Bank of China 1Q Net CNY86.94B Vs. Net CNY84.16B >1398.HK
(08:33 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Operating Income CNY221.98B Vs CNY204.69B
(08:34 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Net Interest Income CNY168.53B Vs CNY156.78B
(08:41 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Net Fee, Commission Income CNY40.92B Vs CNY38.88B
(08:42 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Net Trading Income CNY4.10B Vs CNY2.50B
(08:43 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Net Gains on Financial Investments CNY8.62B Vs CNY5.97B
(08:43 GMT) *ICBC 1Q Credit Impairment Losses CNY69.29B Vs CNY56.97B
(08:44 GMT) ICBC Posts Higher Quarterly Net Profit

By Kosaku Narioka

Industrial & Commercial Bank of China reported a rise in first-quarter net profit.

The Chinese bank said Wednesday that net profit rose 3.3% from a year earlier to 86.94 billion yuan, equivalent to $12.72 billion, for the three months ended March.

The lender said net interest income--the difference between interest earned on loans and that paid on deposits--increased to 168.53 billion yuan from 156.78 billion yuan.

Net fee and commission income was also higher, climbing to 40.92 billion yuan from 38.88 billion yuan.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Taiwan's ASE expects strong demand to boost advanced chip packaging sales in 2026
data.description:
By Wen-Yee Lee

Taiwan’s ASE Technology Holding , the world’s largest chip packaging and testing provider, said on Wednesday it expected revenue from its leading‑edge advanced packaging business to rise 10% to more than $3.5 billion in 2026, as the company sees strong customer demand for AI chips.

CONTEXT AND DETAILS:

  • In February, the company said it expected the business to double to $3.2 billion by 2026.

  • The holding company's subsidiary Siliconware Precision Industries (SPIL) is a major packaging supplier for Nvidia's AI chips.

  • On Wednesday, ASE reported first-quarter revenue of T$173.66 billion ($5.50 billion), up 17.2% from a year earlier, while net income was up 87.3% at T$14.148 billion ($448.22 million).

  • ASE's shares have risen 95% so far this year, far outperforming a 36% rise in the broader market TWSE:TAIEX. The company's shares closed down 1.4% on Wednesday ahead of its earnings release.

  • In April, ASE broke ground on a new chip testing campus in the southern Taiwan city of Kaohsiung, with an investment of more than T$108.3 billion ($3.43 billion), as demand for high-end chips grows. The first phase is scheduled to begin operations in April 2027, with the second phase set to come online in October 2027.

($1 = 31.5650 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: Thyssenkrupp Shares Rise on Kone's Deal to Acquire TK Elevator — Market Talk
data.description: Shares in Thyssenkrupp rise after Finland's Kone agreed to buy TK Elevator for around $23.66 billion, in a deal that would create the world's biggest elevator maker by sales. Thyssenkrupp retains a minority stake in the elevator business following the sale in 2020 to a consortium led by private equity firms Advent, Cinven, and the RAG foundation. Shares trade 9.6% higher at 9.58 euros. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Modon announces sell-out of Tara Park on Reem Island for $544mln
data.description:
Staff Writer

ABU DHABI - Modon has announced the sell-out of Tara Park, a residential development on Reem Island, with the project generating approximately AED2 billion in sales.

The milestone reflects strong investor confidence and sustained demand, underscoring the emirate's growing position as a global safe-haven investment destination.

Tara Park comprises six residential towers with 834 apartments. Connected to Reem Mall, the towers are interlinked by an active podium that connects residents to a wide range of amenities.

“Tara Park further validates Modon’s disciplined, market-driven approach, connecting a prime location and thoughtful placemaking to generate sustainable urban growth and long-term value," said Bill O’Regan, Group CEO of Modon Holding. "We continue to see strong demand across the market, which speaks to the confidence that local and international buyers continue to place in Abu Dhabi, particularly for projects where clear attention to quality of life supports future investment potential.”

Ibrahim Al Maghribi, CEO of Modon Real Estate, said, “The sell-out of Tara Park sends a clear signal. Buyers are not hesitating. Buyers are making considered, long-term decisions. Tara Park was designed with clear buyer priorities and needs in mind, and the market response has validated that approach entirely.”

Article Source: Emirates News Agency (WAM)

data.title: Uzbekistan fund set for $1.95 billion valuation in London IPO
data.description:
Uzbekistan's state investment fund is set for a valuation of around 1.95 billion dollars when it lists in London next month, the fund said in a statement, in what will be the Central Asian country's first initial public offering in Britain.

The Ministry of Economy and Finance of the Republic of Uzbekistan is looking to sell about 30% of the National Investment Fund of the Republic of Uzbekistan (UzNIF) through a dual listing on the Tashkent and London stock exchanges, it said in a statement on Wednesday.

Details:

  • UzNIF's London-listed global depositary receipts (GDRs) will be priced at $25 per share with each GDR representing 64,700 shares.

  • The fund has also applied for its ordinary shares to trade on the Tashkent Stock Exchange.

  • BlackRock, Franklin Resources, Redwheel, and Allan & Gill Gray Foundation have agreed to become cornerstone investors in the deal with commitments to buy around 300 million dollars of GDRs at the offer price, UzNIF said.

  • The IPO will mark the first ⁠time an Uzbek state-backed entity has ​tapped global capital markets.

  • UzNIF was established by presidential ​decree in August 2024 and holds ​stakes ⁠of between 25% and 40% in a number of key state‑owned enterprises.

  • According to ⁠various ​estimates, UzNIF's assets total 2.44 ​billion dollars, Reuters has previously reported.
